market_overview.headline: Markets gained on hopes for US-Iran conflict resolution, with both SPY and QQQ showing bullish but unstacked EMA structure.
market_overview.detail: 43% of stocks in Full Bull mode versus 17% in Full Bear, while SPY and QQQ both trade above EMAs but with compressed spreads suggesting consolidation phase. BTC remains in Bear Rally mode, creating some cross-asset divergence.

momentum_themes.headline: Electronic Technology and Storage Leading Momentum Acceleration
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic technology names dominate the momentum leaderboard with storage/optical leaders SNDK, WDC, STX showing 30%+ gains. Semiconductor equipment and optical networking also accelerating as AI infrastructure demand persists.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Minerals and Tech Hardware Leading, Financials and Tech Services Lagging

strongest.note: Complete sector dominance with all 14 names in bullish structure
Industrial momentum accelerating with 16 of 18 names in Full Bull mode
Hardware and semiconductors leading with strong momentum acceleration

weakest.note: Software and cloud names under pressure with 26 of 32 in bearish structure
Banking sector mixed with many names showing reversal potential
Biotech and healthcare under pressure but some defensive names holding up

sector_analysis.detail: Clear rotation from software/services into hardware and energy as geopolitical tensions favor physical assets and defense. Banking sector showing early reversal signs despite negative net score.

items.text: Technology services sector remains deeply bearish with 81% of names in bear structure
Index EMA spreads compressed suggesting potential consolidation or breakdown risk
BTC divergence from equity strength could signal risk-off rotation ahead
Geopolitical premium may reverse quickly on peace developments

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Markets rallied on hopes for US-Iran conflict resolution despite ongoing tensions, with Trump stating Iran wants to make a deal while maintaining blockade threats. Oracle surged 11% leading a software bounce, while LVMH reported war-related sales impacts highlighting luxury sector vulnerability.

items.text: US-Iran diplomatic hopes drove market gains despite NATO allies refusing to join port blockade
Oracle's 11% pop sparked broader software sector relief rally after AI disruption concerns
Energy sector benefited from continued Middle East tensions and supply disruption fears
Defense contractors gained on sustained geopolitical uncertainty and military spending themes
Luxury goods faced pressure from LVMH's war-impact disclosure, highlighting consumer discretionary risks
